sections.paragraph: an unused root, to be barefoot. (Arab. حَفِىَ id., Syr. ܚܶܦܳܚ unshod, ܐܢܰܚܶܦ to take one’s shoes off. The stock lies in the syllable חף, and the primary idea is that of rubbing off, as if peeling, or barking, see the root חָפַף No. II . Hence حَفِىَ is also, to have the hoof worn down, speaking of cattle, to have the skin galled as a horse. IV. to shave the moustache, to trim the beard.)
